name: maternal-health-sdg-tracker
title: Maternal Health Progress Tracker — SDG 3.1
description: An end-to-end data analysis and machine learning platform that tracks global maternal health progress toward the UN Sustainable Development Goal 3.1 (MMR < 70 per 100,000 live births by 2030). Built with Python, Scikit-learn, Plotly, and Streamlit.

internship.program: IBM Skills Build for University Engagements
internship.problem_statement_title: Tracking Maternal Health Progress Toward SDG 3.1: A Global Data Analysis

sdg_target.description: Reduce global maternal mortality ratio to less than 70 per 100,000 live births by 2030

dashboard_pages.title: Overview Dashboard
dashboard_pages.description: Global MMR trend, KPI cards, income group breakdown, ANC vs MMR scatter plot

dashboard_pages.title: Regional Analysis
dashboard_pages.description: Region-wise bar charts, health indicators radar chart, MMR heatmap over years

dashboard_pages.title: Trend Explorer
dashboard_pages.description: Country-level multi-indicator comparison and correlation heatmap

dashboard_pages.title: ML Predictor
dashboard_pages.description: Interactive form to predict MMR using trained Random Forest model

dashboard_pages.title: Key Insights
dashboard_pages.description: Data-driven policy recommendations, SDG progress gauge, achievement summary

key_findings: Global MMR reduced by ~45% from 2000 to 2023, but progress is highly uneven
Skilled Birth Attendance is the strongest ML predictor of lower maternal mortality
Countries with ANC coverage above 85% consistently achieve lower MMR
Sub-Saharan Africa shows the highest average MMR across all years
Adolescent Birth Rate remains a significant risk factor for elevated MMR
Random Forest outperforms all baselines with R2 above 0.95
future_scope: Real-time WHO / UNICEF / World Bank API integration for live data updates
ARIMA / LSTM time-series forecasting of MMR trends to 2030
